skill.name: longbridge-research
skill.description: Use Longbridge research data and structured workflows to analyze analyst ratings, consensus targets, earnings forecasts, calendars, shareholder data, short interest, IPOs, DeFi yield, and on-chain signals.
skill.summary: Generate Longbridge-backed investment research reports, calendars, ownership views, and market analysis workflows.
skill.author: longbridge

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer reported many high-risk patterns, but the reviewed files are Markdown instructions and command examples, not executable Ruby or cryptographic code. The real risks are intentional Longbridge CLI execution and WebSearch use for market data, including possible account-derived holdings or watchlist context. No prompt injection, malware intent, credential exfiltration, or hidden scripts were found.

medium_findings.title: Intentional External CLI Execution
medium_findings.description: The skill repeatedly instructs the assistant to run Longbridge CLI commands such as help, quote, financial-report, shareholder, consensus, and news commands. This is legitimate for the skill purpose, but it requires command execution in the user environment and may access account-linked market data.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command examples are explicit and central to the workflow. The commands target the Longbridge CLI and are not shell metacharacter payloads, so the risk is operational rather than malicious.

medium_findings.title: Financial Account Context May Be Queried
medium_findings.description: The finance calendar workflow asks the assistant to retrieve holdings and watchlist data from a Longbridge account. This can expose sensitive portfolio context inside the assistant session, even though no evidence shows exfiltration to an unauthorized endpoint.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file directly states that holdings and watchlist are fetched in real time. I found no instruction to send this data outside Longbridge or the user-facing analysis output.

medium_findings.title: WebSearch Used For External Market Data
medium_findings.description: Several workflows require WebSearch for DeFi, on-chain, IPO, and market opportunity data. This is expected for research tasks, but it introduces network dependency and source-quality risk.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The WebSearch requirement is explicit and scoped to public market data. The instructions include source labeling and do not show suspicious domains or credential transfer.

low_findings.title: Static Backtick Execution Findings Are Markdown False Positives
low_findings.description: Most external command detections come from Markdown inline code, fenced bash examples, and tables documenting Longbridge CLI usage. They are not Ruby backtick execution in executable source files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: All reviewed examples are in .md files and appear as documentation or user-facing workflow instructions. No Ruby files or executable backtick evaluation were present in the file tree.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Findings Are Not Confirmed
low_findings.description: The high static weak-cryptography alerts did not correspond to actual cryptographic operations in the reviewed Markdown context. The cited areas describe financial terms, data fields, examples, or command documentation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Reviewed cited locations contain no encryption, hashing, cipher selection, or authentication logic. The finding appears to be keyword-based noise from prose and command documentation.

low_findings.title: Prompt Injection Search Found No Confirmed Attempt
low_findings.description: A targeted review for override, system-update, pre-approval, skip-review, role-change, and similar phrases found no instruction that attempts to control the evaluator or bypass analysis.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Only normal wording such as a market override option was found in reviewed matches. This does not appear to be an attempt to override system or audit instructions.

dangerous_patterns.title: External CLI Commands In Documentation
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill documents commands that an assistant may run in the local environment. Users should review commands before execution, especially when account authorization is active.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The commands are visible and tied to Longbridge data collection. They are not hidden execution payloads, but still represent an external command pattern.

dangerous_patterns.title: Network-Backed Research Workflow
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill asks for WebSearch and public data source retrieval for data not available from Longbridge. This is network access by design and should be disclosed to users.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The network behavior is explicit and limited to market research sources. No suspicious callback endpoint or secret transmission was found.

content.value_statement: Investment research often requires many separate data pulls and consistent report structure. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through Longbridge CLI workflows for ratings, forecasts, ownership, calendars, and research memos.
actual_capabilities: Routes user questions to reference workflows for analyst ratings, EPS forecasts, consensus targets, and finance calendars.
Documents Longbridge CLI commands for shareholder data, fund holders, insider trades, short positions, and industry peers.
Provides report structures for investment proposals, coverage initiation, company profiles, tear sheets, and thesis tracking.
Supports Hong Kong IPO analysis with scoring, source appendices, and data-gap handling rules.
Includes DeFi yield and on-chain analysis workflows that combine Longbridge crypto prices with public research sources.
Requires disclaimers and avoids direct buy or sell instructions in financial outputs.
limitations: The skill depends on the Longbridge CLI or an MCP fallback being available in the user environment.
Some workflows may require Longbridge account authorization and may access holdings or watchlist data.
DeFi, on-chain, IPO, and market trend workflows may need WebSearch for public source data.
It provides research support only and does not produce regulated investment advice.

use_cases.title: Prepare an equity research snapshot
use_cases.description: Collect consensus, valuation, price history, ownership, and recent news into a structured company view.

use_cases.title: Monitor portfolio events
use_cases.description: Review upcoming earnings, dividends, macro events, and market-wide catalysts that may affect watched securities.

use_cases.title: Draft an investment memo
use_cases.description: Use repeatable frameworks for thesis, financial metrics, valuation, catalysts, risks, and data-source notes.

prompt_templates.title: Check analyst sentiment
prompt_templates.prompt: Use Longbridge research to summarize analyst ratings, consensus target price, and recent rating changes for AAPL.US.

prompt_templates.title: Build a company tear sheet
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Longbridge company tear sheet for NVDA.US with business overview, valuation, financial metrics, major shareholders, and recent catalysts.

prompt_templates.title: Compare peers and positioning
prompt_templates.prompt: Analyze 700.HK against its industry peers using Longbridge data. Include valuation, growth, competitive moat, and risks.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare a full investment proposal
prompt_templates.prompt: Draft an investment proposal for TSLA.US using Longbridge data. Cover thesis, financials, valuation, catalysts, downside risks, and data gaps.

output_examples.output: A concise report with symbol normalization, rating distribution, consensus target price, EPS and revenue expectations, revision trend, and source notes.

output_examples.input: What events matter for my watchlist next week?
output_examples.output: A calendar-style summary of earnings, dividends, macro events, market-wide highlights, likely impact areas, and a financial disclaimer.

output_examples.input: Should I participate in a Hong Kong IPO?
output_examples.output: A Hong Kong IPO framework with eligibility checks, four-dimension scoring, prospectus highlights, subscription risk notes, and source appendix.

best_practices: Confirm the exact symbol and market suffix before running Longbridge commands.
Use JSON output where available so the assistant can compare fields consistently.
Keep source notes and data gaps visible in every financial research report.
anti_patterns: Do not treat generated research as a direct buy or sell recommendation.
Do not fabricate missing financial, IPO, DeFi, or on-chain metrics.
Do not run account-linked workflows without understanding what portfolio data may be accessed.

faq.question: Does this skill require a Longbridge account?
faq.answer: Many public data workflows do not require login. Holdings, watchlist, and some account-linked workflows may require authorization.

faq.question: Can it run without the Longbridge CLI?
faq.answer: Some instructions mention MCP fallback. If neither CLI nor MCP is available, the skill can only provide framework guidance.

faq.question: Does it provide investment advice?
faq.answer: No. It structures research and highlights risks, but outputs should remain informational and include disclaimers.

faq.question: Why does it use WebSearch?
faq.answer: Some DeFi, on-chain, IPO, and market trend data is not available through Longbridge and must be sourced publicly.

faq.question: What markets does it cover?
faq.answer: The references include US, Hong Kong, A-share, Singapore, crypto spot symbols, and Hong Kong IPO workflows where supported by data.

faq.question: What are the main security considerations?
faq.answer: The skill may run local Longbridge commands, use web research, and access account context when authorized.
